response:
blank The living room function for LED output is derived by subtracting natural light from the target illumination. blank To maintain exactly 500 lumens, the LED output \( L(t) \) must satisfy \( L(t) + N(t) = 500 \). blank Substituting the given natural light function yields \( L(t) = 500 - (300 + 200\sin(\frac{\pi}{12}t)) \). blank Simplifying this expression results in \( L(t) = 200 - 200\sin(\frac{\pi}{12}t) \). blank Since the sine function oscillates between -1 and 1, \( L(t) \) ranges from 0 to 400 lumens, which fits within the 0 to 500 lumen capacity. blank Total energy consumption is calculated by integrating the power usage over 24 hours. blank Power consumption in watts is \( 0.1 \times L(t) \), so energy in watt-hours is the integral of \( 0.1 \times (200 - 200\sin(\frac{\pi}{12}t)) \) from \( t=0 \) to \( t=24 \). blank The integral of the constant term \( 200 \) over 24 hours is \( 4800 \). blank The integral of the sine term over a full period (24 hours) is zero. blank Therefore, the total lumen-hours is 4800. blank Multiplying by the rate of 0.1 watts per lumen-hour gives a total energy consumption of 480 watt-hours.

blank The bedroom lighting requires finding all integer triplets \( (r, g, b) \) where each value is between 0 and 255. blank The constraint equation is \( 0.3r + 0.6g + 0.1b = 500 \). blank Multiplying the entire equation by 10 to clear decimals gives \( 3r + 6g + b = 5000 \). blank Solving for \( b \) yields \( b = 5000 - 3r - 6g \). blank Valid combinations exist only when \( 0 \le b \le 255 \). blank This implies \( 0 \
